overview.introduction: Continuous Integration focusing on automated linting, compilation safety, and unit tests integration.
overview.why_it_matters: Understanding Continuous Integration allows developers to deliver reliable software iterations safely and iteratively.
overview.industry_usage: Adopted by organizations globally to reduce manual errors and scale deployments frequencies.
overview.what_you_will_build: A configured local Git repository with a custom pipeline file running a local pipeline execution simulator.
learning_outcomes: Explain the purpose and mechanics of Continuous Integration.
Write pipeline stages lists in YAML configurations.
Incorporate validation gates like linting, unit tests, and compilation checks.
Simulate manual approval gates and post-deploy rollbacks.
Analyze and debug running pipeline logs.

key_concepts.title: Compilation & Quality Checks
key_concepts.definition: Automated compiler executions and syntax audits evaluate code logic safety on every push command.
key_concepts.explanation: A pipeline splits software delivery tasks into sequential stages, ensuring check parameters are validated.

commands.name: run-pipeline
commands.purpose: Simulate and run pipeline config stages.
commands.output_explanation: Executes local stages configured inside pipeline.yml.

key_concepts.pro_tip: Keep pipelines modular to easily pinpoint error spots and reuse build structures.
key_concepts.best_practices: Ensure test checks are executed early in the pipeline timeline to capture anomalies quickly.
key_concepts.common_mistakes: Skipping compilation check dependencies when executing deploy pipeline triggers.
key_concepts.real_world_usage: CI/CD checks compile code and execute test cases for every developer push command.
key_concepts.summary: Continuous Integration forms the foundation of devops lifecycle automation.

interactive_examples.command:
echo -e 'stages:
  - checkout
  - lint
  - test
  - build' > pipeline.yml
interactive_examples.common_mistakes: Typing stage names incorrectly in config schemas.

lessons.title: 1. Create Configuration (Define lint stage)
lessons.instruction: Add a 'lint' stage name to your stages configuration in 'pipeline.yml'.
lessons.example:
echo -e 'stages:
  - checkout
  - lint
  - test
  - build' > pipeline.yml
lessons.hint:
Run command: echo -e 'stages:
  - checkout
  - lint
  - test
  - build' > pipeline.yml
lessons.solution:
echo -e 'stages:
  - checkout
  - lint
  - test
  - build' > pipeline.yml

lessons.title: 2. Inspect Status
lessons.instruction: Run git status to see modified files.

lessons.title: 3. Audit History Log
lessons.explanation: Displays timeline log of previous commits.
lessons.instruction: Print commit history log to console screen.

lessons.title: 5. Show pointer references
lessons.explanation: Locates branches and tags SHA mappings.
lessons.instruction: Query refs mapping by running git show-ref.

lessons.title: 6. Show active branches
lessons.instruction: List all branches using git branch.

lessons.title: Mini Challenge: CI Verification
lessons.definition: No step-by-step instructions are provided here. Execute this task directly.
lessons.explanation: Complete this task on your own.
lessons.instruction: Author a CI pipeline definition with checkout, lint, test, and build stages in 'pipeline.yml'.
lessons.example:
echo -e 'stages:
  - checkout
  - lint
  - test
  - build' > pipeline.yml && git add pipeline.yml && git commit -m 'feat: add lint stage'
lessons.hint:
Run command: echo -e 'stages:
  - checkout
  - lint
  - test
  - build' > pipeline.yml && git add pipeline.yml && git commit -m 'feat: add lint stage'
lessons.solution:
echo -e 'stages:
  - checkout
  - lint
  - test
  - build' > pipeline.yml && git add pipeline.yml && git commit -m 'feat: add lint stage'

exercises.title: Configure local pipeline
exercises.problem: Practice pipeline configuration by configuring pipeline.yml.

quiz.question: What is the primary function of CI/CD Continuous Integration?
options: To automate software integration and delivery pipelines.
To assign cloud infrastructure access permissions.
To coordinate cluster pods networking schedules.
quiz.answer: To automate software integration and delivery pipelines.
quiz.explanation: CI/CD automates integration testing, packaging, and deployments.

quiz.question: What is the role of the Continuous Integration phase?
options: To automate code linting, checks, and unit tests runs.
To store backups of configuration parameters.
quiz.answer: To automate code linting, checks, and unit tests runs.
quiz.explanation: Continuous Integration automatically merges developer code commits and validates them using automated quality checks.

quiz.question: What distinguishes Continuous Delivery from Continuous Deployment?
options: Continuous Delivery requires a manual approval trigger to deploy.
Continuous Delivery uses no YAML configurations.
Continuous Deployment runs no unit tests.
quiz.answer: Continuous Delivery requires a manual approval trigger to deploy.
quiz.explanation: Continuous Delivery packages releases automatically but keeps deployment staging/production behind manual approval checks.

quiz.question: What keyword defines the sequential blocks in a pipeline schema?
quiz.explanation: The 'stages' key organizes pipeline execution components into sequential phases.

quiz.question: Why is testing early in the pipeline timeline recommended?
options: To discover software anomalies early before costly downstream phases run.
To speed up container packaging durations.
quiz.answer: To discover software anomalies early before costly downstream phases run.
quiz.explanation: Running test cases early avoids pushing broken packages downstream (known as shifting security/testing left).

quiz.question: Which stage compiles source files into usable packages?
quiz.explanation: The 'build' stage compiles code dependencies and produces deployable software assets.

quiz.question: Which parameter stores sensitive keys safely inside pipelines?
quiz.explanation: Secrets securely inject access credentials without printing them inside execution logs outputs.

quiz.question: What is a manual approval gate?
options: An intentional halt in a pipeline requiring admin click approvals to proceed.
An error during file packaging runs.
quiz.answer: An intentional halt in a pipeline requiring admin click approvals to proceed.
quiz.explanation: Approval gates ensure human oversight validates build status before packages deploy to staging/production.

quiz.question: What is a pipeline rollback?
options: Restoring the previous working version after a deploy failure.
quiz.answer: Restoring the previous working version after a deploy failure.
quiz.explanation: Rollbacks revert clusters or systems back to stable states in the event of new deployment failures.

quiz.question: What format is typically used to write pipeline configuration layouts?
quiz.explanation: YAML is standard for describing pipelines stages, triggers, and commands configurations.

resources.summary: Learn more about CI/CD Continuous Integration through official documentation, guides, and cheat sheets.

links.title: CI/CD Continuous Integration Guide
links.description: Official documentation and manual for Continuous Integration.

links.title: CI/CD Awesome References
links.description: Collection of tutorials, best practices, and pipeline configs.

resources.cheat_sheet:
stages:
  - checkout       # Stage 1
  - test           # Stage 2
  - build          # Stage 3
  - deploy         # Stage 4
revision_notes: Pipelines are configured as stages lists in pipeline.yml.
Rollbacks revert system packages to clean previous release states.
Manual approvals introduce checkpoints for production releases.
best_practices: Execute syntax and lint checkers first.
Do not store passwords in plaintext.
beginner_mistakes: Hardcoding server IPs in YAML files.
Excluding unit testing checks from integration hooks.

interview_questions.question: What is CI/CD Continuous Integration?
interview_questions.answer: It is an automation lifecycle framework delivering repeatable, quality-checked code iterations.

books.title: Continuous Delivery — Jez Humble & David Farley
books.description: The definitive book explaining reliable software releases configurations, packaging, and deployments.
